book_claims.claim: Low-protein, high-complex-carbohydrate diet produces maximum longevity and health in mice
book_claims.mechanism: Does not activate IGF-1/TOR-S6K/PKA pro-aging pathways as strongly as protein or simple sugars
book_claims.study_ref: Solon-Biet SM et al., Cell Metabolism 2014 (ch4-8)

book_claims.claim: Whole grains recommended as a complex carbohydrate source; part of Sardinian and Molochio centenarian diets
book_claims.mechanism: High fiber slows glucose absorption; provides B vitamins, minerals, and sustained energy

book_claims.claim: Complex carbohydrates support healthy weight management comparable to low-carb diets without muscle or water loss
book_claims.mechanism: Adequate carbs preserve glycogen and muscle protein; low-carb diets cause loss of water and lean mass
book_claims.study_ref: Yang MU, Van Itallie TB, Journal of Clinical Investigation 1976 (ch4-21)

book_claims.claim: Maximizing complex carbohydrates from whole grains while reducing pasta, rice, and bread and minimizing sugar is recommended for diabetes prevention
book_claims.mechanism: Complex carbohydrates cause a slower, lower insulin response than simple sugars and refined starches

supplementary_research.finding: Whole grain consumption including ancient wheat varieties is associated with reduced all-cause mortality and lower risk of type 2 diabetes and cardiovascular disease (PMID 22716912)
Emmer/farro wheat has a higher protein quality, more dietary fiber, and a lower glycemic index than modern soft wheat (PMID 27301975), supporting its use as a longevity-aligned grain
Farro provides 7 g protein and 3.5 g fiber per 45 g dry serving alongside magnesium, zinc, and B vitamins; its lower glycemic response compared to refined grains supports metabolic health

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Includes arabinoxylan and beta-glucan; feeds beneficial gut bacteria and slows glucose absorption
Important cofactor for >300 enzymes; absorption aided by adequate vitamin D
Bioavailability improved by soaking or sprouting grains to reduce phytate
Supports NAD+ synthesis; critical for mitochondrial energy metabolism

synergies.description: Farro and legumes form a complete amino acid profile together — the classic Italian farrotto e fagioli combination mirrors centenarian Sardinian and Calabrian cuisine
Olive oil's healthy monounsaturated fats slow gastric emptying, further reducing the glycemic response to farro
Vitamin C in leafy greens enhances absorption of non-heme iron and zinc from farro
